2606.26669.title: SKILL-DISCO: Distilling and Compiling Agent Traces into Reusable Procedural Skills
2606.26669.paper_summary: Agents often repeatedly solve similar task instances from scratch, leading to unnecessary reasoning cost and long execution traces. Prior work has explored workflow reuse and executable skill induction, but it remains unclear which task scenarios admit procedural skills and how the shared procedural structure should be represented across successful traces. We study this problem in FSM-defined scenarios, where successful traces can be viewed as paths in an unknown transition graph, and formulate procedural skills as reusable parameterized control-flow subgraphs. Based on this view, we introduce SkillDisCo, a distillation-and-compilation framework that distills reusable PFSM subgraphs from successful traces and compiles them into callable, executable, and verifiable procedural skills. Experiments on ALFWorld and WebArena show that SkillDisCo improves success rates and reduces agent turns across benchmarks and model scales, demonstrating the benefits of representing shared experience as reusable execution structures.
2606.26669.paper_summary_zh: 代理人經常從頭開始重複解決類似的任務實例，導致不必要的推理成本和冗長的執行痕跡。先前的研究探討了工作流程重用和可執行技能的引導，但仍不清楚哪些任務場景允許程序技能，以及如何在成功的痕跡中表示共享的程序結構。我們在FSM定義的場景中研究這個問題，成功的痕跡可以被視為未知轉換圖中的路徑，並將程序技能公式化為可重用的參數化控制流子圖。基於這一觀點，我們引入了SkillDisCo，一個提煉和編譯框架，從成功的痕跡中提煉可重用的PFSM子圖，並將其編譯成可調用、可執行和可驗證的程序技能。在ALFWorld和WebArena上的實驗顯示，SkillDisCo提高了成功率並減少了代理人的回合數，展示了將共享經驗表示為可重用執行結構的好處。
2606.26669.author: Zhongxin Guo et.al.
2606.26669.authors: Zhongxin Guo, Danrui Qi, Hanwen Gu, Peng Cheng, Yongqiang Xiong
